apex_basket.value_chain_position: Financial-management platform layer — QuickBooks is the ~85%-share SMB accounting/payroll OS, TurboTax dominates assisted tax, Credit Karma is the consumer financial-data network; the operating layer for small-business finance.
apex_basket.thesis: The highest-conviction guard-clean compounder in the universe: an 85%-share SMB-finance OS with an expanding-margin moat and a still-positive margin-of-safety, the anchor of the book.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails toll on virtually every US SMB; QuickBooks Online international + Credit Karma monetization keep secular demand growing 14-15% with no single-customer dependence.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: company-wide gross margin ~78%->80.8% (FY23->FY25) on +14% 3yr CAGR, non-GAAP operating margin +450bps to 58% — pricing power, not commoditization (one watch-item: TurboTax-DIY 'lost on price' to free/AI, masked by TurboTax-Live mix +36%).
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: ROIC ~12% and rising, $7.7B TTM FCF (37% FCF margin) redeployed into Credit Karma/AI; rule-of-40 = 51.9 with capex-light SaaS economics.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +64% / ev_gp 4.88 vs +15% rev — guard passes clean (cheapest gross-profit multiple among the apex), no deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — diversified Fortune-500-to-SMB base, 85% QuickBooks share, debate verdict B / value_conviction 5.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Veterinary-diagnostics toll-taker — in-clinic analyzers + recurring consumable test kits + a direct reference-lab network; a razor/blade installed base that is deeply sticky once placed.
apex_basket.thesis: An orthogonal, guard-clean compounder with 36% ROIC and an expanding-margin razor/blade moat — the book's diversifier away from the AI-capex line.
apex_basket.theme_durability: genomics_bio_tools / companion-animal diagnostics — secular pet-health spend, no scaled public competitor, durable consumable annuity per placed instrument.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: comparable gross margin 61.8%->63.4% (FY25->Q1'26, +90bps) on +10-12% organic revenue — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite 36% ROIC (return on incremental capital), +10-12% organic with double-digit international runway, rule-of-40 = 37.4 and 24% FCF margin.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +9.6% / ev_gp 16.9x vs +13% rev — guard passes (price ~$561 sits below base SoP ~$615); no deduction.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; no AI-capex, China, or single-customer exposure.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Global payment-network operator — VisaNet connects ~15,000 issuers to ~100M merchant locations, taking a per-transaction toll on every card-based payment globally; the canonical fintech-rails chokepoint.
apex_basket.thesis: A load-bearing duopoly rail with retained pricing power and a VAS software re-rate optionality, orthogonal to the AI-capex line and sized for ballast.
apex_basket.theme_durability: fintech_rails — duopoly network effect no entrant has broken at scale; net revenue accelerated +9%->+17% across FY2Q25-FY2Q26, ~30% VAS software mix compounding.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: ~65-67% GAAP operating margin retained while net revenue accelerated and EPS +35% latest-Q — pricing power retained even as lower-margin VAS/Visa-Direct mixed in.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 49% FCF margin, $21.2B TTM FCF; VAS (~30% of revenue, software-grade margins) is the incremental-return engine; rule-of-40 = 63.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$300-305 sits just below the ~$320 quote (~-5%); guard passes with no size penalty — quality ballast, not a deep-value bet.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, STRENGTHENING; the structural watch-item is agentic/stablecoin rail-bypass, an overhang not yet evidence.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Vertically integrated uranium miner/processor — Tier-1 assets (Cigar Lake, McArthur River) plus conversion and the 49% Westinghouse stake; the nuclear-fuel chokepoint for the existing fleet and the SMR build-out.
apex_basket.thesis: The highest-quality public uranium toll-taker and a clean orthogonal leg — nuclear-fuel scarcity that does not ride the hyperscaler capex line, kept at a managed size while GM re-expands.
apex_basket.theme_durability: energy_transition / nuclear-fuel supply — long-term contract prices re-pricing to the highest since 2008 (~$90/lb), AI-baseload demand + signed ~$80B US-gov Westinghouse program; load_bearing 5, interrogator 5.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector AMBER: consolidated gross margin COMPRESSED 33.9%->26.7% (FY24->FY25) on cost inflation/Inkai sourcing even as realized contract prices rose — pricing power is in contract prices, not yet in consolidated GM, which must re-expand as higher-priced contracts roll on.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 25% FCF margin, ~C$1.9B FY25 adj-EBITDA, net-near-flat leverage (-0.1x); incremental returns ride re-pricing of the long-term contract book plus Westinghouse distributions; rule-of-40 = 32.9.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$95-100 sits at the ~$101 quote (~flat); guard passes but the amber GM keeps size disciplined.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, contract-backlog re-pricing; commodity/uranium-spot and GM-compression are the watch-items.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Spine-surgery implants + the ExcelsiusGPS robotic-navigation platform — capital equipment drives per-procedure implant consumable pull-through; the largest pure-play spine platform post-NuVasive.
apex_basket.thesis: A guard-clean robotics leg with an expanding-margin implant annuity, diversifying the book into surgical robotics at a still-positive margin-of-safety.
apex_basket.theme_durability: robotics_automation / surgical robotics — ExcelsiusGPS taking share from Medtronic Mazor and Stryker Mako-spine; the robot is the razor that pulls through high-margin implants; load_bearing 4.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin ~67.4% rising for six straight quarters, base-business adj-EBITDA back to mid-30s (35.7% Q4'25) on +16.7% revenue — pricing power, not commoditization.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: ROIC ~6.2% (depressed by NuVasive integration, normalizing), net cash (-0.5x), $605M TTM FCF; incremental returns ride robot placements -> implant pull-through; rule-of-40 = 43.0.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +12% / ev_gp 5.11x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (one of the cheapest gross-profit multiples in the book).
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — STRENGTHENING, interrogator 4; watch robot-placement competition (Medtronic Stealth AXiS / Stryker Mako-spine) and the 6.2% ROIC normalization.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: AI-accelerator silicon + the CUDA software platform — the dominant compute layer every LLM training/inference workload must run on; ~80-90% of AI-training revenue.
apex_basket.thesis: The most load-bearing chokepoint in the universe and the compute leg of the AI-capex axis; held only at a combined-capped size because it shares the hyperscaler-capex driver with TSM and VRT.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — DC revenue +142% YoY, top-5 hyperscalers ~50%+ of DC revenue (the concentration tell); secular but the demand is one capex line that can digest.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: non-GAAP gross margin rose to ~75% (Q1'27) WHILE revenue grew +114% then +65% — durable pricing power into the largest ramp in tech history.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: Elite ~45% ROIC on incremental capital, 47% FCF margin, $119B TTM FCF, net cash; capex-light fabless model redeploys at extreme returns; rule-of-40 = 117.6.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos +2.3% — base SoP ~$210 sits on top of the ~$205 price, i.e. essentially NO discount; guard does not veto but argues for size discipline; peak_flag true (the air-pocket case).
apex_basket.growth_durability: high but cycle-sensitive — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5; a 2-quarter hyperscaler digestion pause is the central risk and the reason for the combined cap.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Advanced-fab layer — the world's sole source of leading-edge logic (3nm/2nm) that every AI accelerator, custom ASIC and smartphone AP must be built on; ~100% of sub-5nm capacity.
apex_basket.thesis: The supply-chain chokepoint of the entire AI build-out and the foundry leg of the AI-capex axis; combined-capped because a hyperscaler-capex pause and the Taiwan single-point-of-failure correlate it with NVDA and VRT.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure — revenue +31% YoY, N2 sold out, sole-source leading edge; Intel 18A years behind, Samsung trails on yield; the most structural moat in the book.
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin 54.4%->59.9%->66.2% (FY23->Q1'26) on +36% USD revenue — rising scarcity rent / pricing power, only deliberate overseas-fab + N2-ramp dilution as offsets.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 27% FCF margin on a heavy-capex model but capex efficiency is improving as leading-edge ASPs rise; $1.1T-NT TTM FCF, net cash; rule-of-40 = 57.8.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: Base SoP ~$1,180/ADR sits ABOVE the ~$424 ADR quote (the SoP capitalizes local EPS x ADR ratio); guard passes, but exposure to the AI-capex axis caps size, not price.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high — load_bearing 5, interrogator 5, conviction 4; customer-concentration + Taiwan geopolitical single-point-of-failure are the structural tails behind the combined cap.

apex_basket.value_chain_position: Data-center power and thermal-management infrastructure — power conversion, UPS, precision and liquid cooling that every AI/HPC data center must install; a genuine chokepoint in the AI build-out.
apex_basket.thesis: The power-and-cooling leg of the AI-capex axis and the most guard-clean of the three AI-infra chokepoints; combined-capped and sized smallest because it shares the hyperscaler-capex driver with NVDA and TSM.
apex_basket.theme_durability: ai_infrastructure + energy_transition — ~70% of revenue tied to data-center deployments, +29% YoY on hyperscaler pull; load_bearing 5, but the entire thesis rides a handful of buyers (the capex tell).
apex_basket.moat_evidence: Lie-detector GREEN: gross margin ~31%->34.4% (FY23->FY25) and adjusted operating margin 18.5%->23.2% across 2025 even through the tariff dip — expanding GM on +27.7% revenue = real pricing power.
apex_basket.reinvestment_runway: 21% FCF margin, ROIC ~10.5% and rising, $2.3B TTM FCF; >$15B binding backlog funds reinvestment; rule-of-40 = 50.2.
apex_basket.valuation_guard: sop_mos -7.6% — price ~$303 brackets the base SoP ~$300 (cleanest guard of the AI-capex legs); no veto, size held by the combined cap.
apex_basket.growth_durability: high but cycle-sensitive — STRENGTHENING, interrogator 5; hyperscaler-capex concentration is the explicit risk and the reason for the combined cap.

runner_ups.thesis: Guard-clean enterprise-workflow + AI-agent orchestration toll (GM flat-high 77.5%, op-margin expanding, sop_mos +15.5); excluded ONLY by the 3-per-Technology GICS cap, first off the bench if a Tech slot frees.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +15.5% / ev_gp 9.9x vs +22% rev — guard passes clean.

runner_ups.thesis: Cheap (ev_gp 6.8), guard-clean (sop_mos +15.7) industrial-software/PLM toll with an expanding ~84% gross margin; bench behind the Technology GICS cap.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +15.7% / ev_gp 6.77x vs +28% rev — guard passes clean.

runner_ups.thesis: 91%-GM AEC/manufacturing CAD workflow monopoly, deepest margin-of-safety in Tech (sop_mos +32%, ev_gp 6.95); bench behind the Technology GICS cap.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +32.3% / ev_gp 6.95x vs +18% rev — guard passes clean (bear-SoP ~$212 sits above the ~$198 price).

runner_ups.thesis: Guard-clean (sop_mos +24.9) restaurant-vertical payment OS, GM expanding with net income inflected positive; orthogonal to AI-capex but blocked by the Technology GICS cap behind INTU/NVDA/TSM.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +24.9% / ev_gp 7.56x vs +23% rev — guard passes clean (bear SoP $22 net-cash-floored, ~-10%).

runner_ups.thesis: Deep guard pass (sop_mos +66, bear SoP ~$62 ABOVE the ~$47 price) PFA/electrophysiology + robotic-bronchoscopy grower; held off the apex because management GUIDED FY26 gross margin lower (first compression after years of expansion = lie-detector amber) and trajectory is PIVOTING.
runner_ups.valuation_guard: sop_mos +66.3% / ev_gp 5.84x — guard passes deep, but amber GM guide caps enthusiasm.

disruptor_memo.rubric_and_weighting: Four pillars ~25 pts each applied ONLY to names clearing the gates: (1) theme durability & load-bearing position, (2) moat & pricing power with the gross-margin trajectory as the lie-detector, (3) reinvestment runway & incremental ROIC, (4) growth-adjusted valuation as a GUARD ONLY. Pillars 1-3 set the ranking; valuation (sop_mos_pct, ev_gp, rule_of_40, peak_flag) acted purely as a guard that could deduct, cap, or veto, and was NEVER the reason one name ranked above another that passed the guard clean. This is the FIRST disruptor apex basket — no prior slate exists (no pre-existing apex_basket_disruptor.json was found before this write), so the rise/fall section is initialization-only.
disruptor_memo.gate_results: All 40 debated names cleared the hard gates as pre-stamped and re-verified: every name is ttm_fcf_positive=true, rev_growth_gate=true, funded_solvency != weak (all strong/moderate/exempt_financial), and forensic_gate is empty (no EXCLUDE, no CAP). No name was rendered ineligible by a gate, so differentiation came entirely from the rubric and the valuation guard.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_forensic_gate: None — no name carried forensic_gate=EXCLUDE or CAP.
disruptor_memo.excluded_by_hard_gates: None on FCF/growth/solvency — all 40 passed. (BLLN, BE, IOT, NVT, TOST etc. are FCF-positive on a TTM basis; no fcf_inflecting-only name had to be carried, so the <=0.5 inflecting rule did not bind.)
disruptor_memo.deducted_or_vetoed_by_valuation_guard: Guard VETO / size-0.5 territory (sop_mos_pct <= -40%, the CRO cannot get near the price) removed: ASML (-99.9%), BE (-81.7%), PLTR (-79.3%), HWM (-72.2%), ARM (-54.0%), WWD (-53.5%). Guard DEDUCTED (price above base SoP, -20% to -30%) and kept off the apex: ANET (-26.5), CRDO (-22.3), APP (-21.5), FICO (-21.1, also verdict C/conviction 2), GE (-28.4, C), ISRG (-29.5, C), HEI (-20.1), MEDP (-20.3, C + rising customer concentration into cancelling metabolic names), WST (-22.4, C). Guard-CLEAN names that still missed the 8 purely on the GICS-sector cap (not on the guard): NOW (+15.5), PTC (+15.7), ADSK (+32.3), TOST (+24.9) — all Technology, blocked behind INTU/NVDA/TSM; these populate the runner-ups.
disruptor_memo.fcf_inflecting_keeps: None. No apex pick is fcf_inflecting; every one is ttm_fcf_positive=true on a trailing basis, so no name required the guidance/backlog FCF-turn evidence or the <=0.5 size cap on that basis.
disruptor_memo.rise_fall_vs_prior_apex: FIRST disruptor apex — there is no prior basket to compare against (this run created apex_basket_disruptor.json for the first time). Initialization slate (8): INTU 1.20, IDXX 1.15, V 1.05, CCJ 1.05, GMED 0.95, NVDA 0.85, TSM 0.80, VRT 0.65 (total 7.70 units). All eight are NEW positions by definition. Bench (runner-ups): NOW, PTC, ADSK, TOST, BSX.

theme_concentration_stress.ai-capex (MANDATORY check): CARRIES 3 names — NVDA (compute), TSM (foundry/supply-chain), VRT (power & cooling) — the three legs of the SAME hyperscaler-capex line; a 2-quarter digestion pause hits all three at once. RESOLVED = kept-with-cap (not diversified out, because these are the three highest-quality load-bearing chokepoints in the universe and the book would lose its AI exposure entirely without them). Combined cap: {names:[NVDA,TSM,VRT], max_units:2.3, axis:'ai-capex'} = 29.9% of the 7.70-unit book, under the 30% ceiling, so a single hyperscaler capex cut cannot impair >30% of the book. Sizes were deliberately stepped down (0.85/0.80/0.65) below the guard-clean compounders to enforce this. The ai_infrastructure THEME id also independently caps at 3 names / 29.9% weight — both constraints bind here and both are satisfied.
theme_concentration_stress.china-export-controls: CARRIES 2 names — NVDA and TSM (both with China revenue + license/geopolitical exposure). RESOLVED = covered by the same ai-capex combined cap (both are members), so a single export-control ruling is bounded by the 2.3-unit / 30% ceiling; no separate swap required since the cap already limits the overlapping pair.
theme_concentration_stress.supply-chain-tsmc: CARRIES 2 names — TSM (the foundry itself) and NVDA (fabbed at TSMC); a Taiwan/advanced-node disruption hits both. RESOLVED = bounded by the same ai-capex combined cap (both members), within the 30% ceiling.
theme_concentration_stress.single-customer-hyperscaler: CARRIES 3 names — NVDA, TSM, VRT all ultimately depend on the same handful of hyperscaler buyers. RESOLVED = identical membership to the ai-capex cap, so the 2.3-unit / 30% ceiling already constrains it; no additional name shares this top-customer set outside the capped trio.
theme_concentration_stress.rate-duration: CARRIES 2+ names — INTU and V (long-duration growth/quality multiples that compress together when real rates rise). RESOLVED = kept WITHOUT a numeric combined cap because they sit in DIFFERENT GICS sectors (Technology vs Financial Services) and different sub-drivers (SMB-finance software vs payment network), are both guard-clean (sop_mos +64 / base-SoP-at-spot), and together are 29.2% of the book — already under 30% via the fintech_rails theme cap, which binds first. A rate shock is a multiple event, not a thesis break, for both.
theme_concentration_stress.china-revenue (separate from export-cont: No additional >=2-name axis: the only material China-revenue names in the apex are the already-capped NVDA/TSM pair.
theme_concentration_stress.energy_transition: CARRIES 2 names — CCJ (uranium fuel) and VRT (2nd theme, data-center power). These are orthogonal sub-drivers (nuclear-fuel-cycle vs hyperscaler power-cooling) and sum to 22.1% of the book, comfortably under 30%; VRT's correlated risk is already governed by the ai-capex combined cap, so no separate energy cap is needed.

disruptor_memo.construction_note: The book deliberately leans into guard-CLEAN compounders (INTU +64, IDXX +9.6, GMED +12, V at-spot, CCJ at-spot) for the larger sizes and holds the three AI-capex chokepoints (NVDA/TSM/VRT) only at a combined-capped, stepped-down weight. The richest hypergrowth disruptors (ARM, ASML, PLTR, APP, CRDO, HWM, WWD, BE) were vetoed or deducted by the valuation guard despite strong theme stories, consistent with the System-of-Record rule that valuation is a guard the great theme cannot override. GICS sector caps bind harder than theme caps for Technology and are the sole reason NOW/PTC/ADSK/TOST sit in the runner-ups rather than the apex.

stress_test.note: Market-based stress: weighted basket return to the 52-week lows, and to 52w-lows -15% (recession). cro_bear is the agents' own adverse SoP (bear_fv_px, theme-pause case); when missing or implying upside it is flagged invalid and the published downside is the market-based recession stress.

correlation.fx_note: betas vs SMH (semis) + QQQ (long-duration tech) — the AI-capex/rate-duration systematic axes.

pool_stats.banner: Highest-volatility sleeve: 8 profitable secular-theme names from a 40-name thematic screen (taxonomy v1.0). Long-duration growth multiples — expect drawdowns ~2x the value book's; sized as the SMALLEST sleeve by design and never blended into the Apex or Value NAVs.
